## What this changes

Briefly describe the change and the layer it touches (phase / tool / knowledge / docs).

## Why

The engineering or usability reason. Link any related issue.

## Checklist

- [ ] Change is backward compatible (or the break is called out and justified).
- [ ] Improves an existing file where possible rather than adding a new one.
- [ ] Tests pass: `python3 tests/test_*.py` (and `tools/test_*.py` if `tools/` changed).
- [ ] Docs updated to match (README / handbook / relevant `docs/` or `architecture/`).
- [ ] No secrets, tokens, or machine-local paths committed.
- [ ] Every new claim traces to a real artifact, standard, or code path (see `CONTRIBUTING.md`).
